preamble.summary: Isaac delivers additional final instructions. Jacob receives the books of Abraham containing agricultural laws, festivals, and covenant obligations. The formal division of the inheritance between Jacob and Esau is recorded.
preamble.remarkable: The 'books of Abraham' establish a literary chain: Abraham wrote, Isaac preserved, Jacob received. This supports Jubilees' own claim to be ancient, revealed literature.
preamble.friction: The existence of 'books of Abraham' has no basis in the canonical Torah.
preamble.connections: Genesis 25:5-6 (inheritance); Genesis 35:29 (Isaac's death); Jubilees 21 (Abraham's testament to Isaac).

verses.text_reference: And in the sixth year Jacob sent his sons to pasture their sheep.
verses.rendering: In the sixth year of this week, Jacob sent his sons and servants to pasture their sheep. Esau gathered his sons and separated from Jacob.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob and Esau were divided in their possessions.
verses.rendering: Jacob and Esau divided their possessions. Esau departed from the land of Canaan to Edom and settled on Mount Seir.

verses.text_reference: And Esau saw that the land of Canaan was given to Jacob by inheritance.
verses.rendering: Esau saw that the land of Canaan had been given to his brother Jacob as an inheritance. He accepted this and went his way.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob dwelt in Canaan, as the Lord had commanded Abraham.
verses.rendering: Jacob settled in the land of Canaan, as the Lord had commanded his grandfather Abraham.

verses.text_reference: And he gave him all the books of Abraham.
verses.rendering: Isaac gave Jacob all the books of Abraham and the books of his forefathers. Jacob read and studied them with great care.
translator_notes: The transmission of sacred books from patriarch to patriarch is a central motif in Jubilees.

key_terms.note: Patriarchal writings transmitted through the generations, legitimizing Jubilees' own pseudepigraphic authority

verses.text_reference: And Jacob wrote a copy for himself, and gave the original to Levi.
verses.rendering: Jacob made a copy for himself and kept the books. He gave the originals to his son Levi, to preserve and pass on to the children of Israel.
translator_notes: Levi as custodian of sacred literature reflects the priestly class's role as teachers and scribes.

verses.text_reference: And when Isaac died, Esau and Jacob buried him in the double cave.
verses.rendering: When Isaac died, Esau and Jacob buried him in the double cave near their father Abraham. They wept for him.
They mourned for Isaac. Esau mourned his father. After the days of mourning ended, Esau departed for Edom.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob sent to Esau gifts, and peace was between them.
verses.rendering: Jacob sent Esau gifts of cattle, sheep, and donkeys, and there was peace between them.

verses.text_reference: And Esau rejected the Canaan portion and took the land of Edom.
verses.rendering: Esau set aside the portion his father had given him in Canaan and took instead the land of Edom.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob inherited the whole land of Canaan.
verses.rendering: Jacob inherited the entire land of Canaan along with his father Isaac's blessings.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob gave Esau a further gift, for he loved his brother.
verses.rendering: Jacob gave Esau an additional gift, for he loved his brother.

verses.text_reference: And Esau rejoiced and departed in peace.
verses.rendering: Esau rejoiced over the gifts and departed from him in peace.

verses.text_reference: And they were separated, for the inheritance was too great for them to dwell together.
verses.rendering: They separated from each other, for their combined wealth was too great for them to live in the same place.

verses.text_reference: And Esau went to Mount Seir.
verses.rendering: The land of Canaan could not sustain them both. So Esau went to Mount Seir.
Jacob settled in the hills of Hebron and worshiped the Lord with all his heart.

verses.text_reference: And the days of Isaac were one hundred and eighty years.
verses.rendering: Isaac's lifespan was one hundred and eighty years. He died in the forty-fourth jubilee, in its sixth year.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob buried his father Isaac beside Abraham.
verses.rendering: Jacob buried his father Isaac in the double cave in the land of Canaan, beside his father Abraham.
